pairs.question: What was the Detroit Pistons' win-loss record for the 2000–01 NBA season?
pairs.answer: The Detroit Pistons finished the 2000–01 NBA season with a record of 32 wins and 50 losses.

pairs.question: Who was the Detroit Pistons' top scorer during the 2000–01 season, and what were their season averages?
pairs.answer: Jerry Stackhouse was the Pistons' top scorer, averaging 29.8 points and 5.1 assists per game during the season.

pairs.question: What trades did the Detroit Pistons make during the 2000–01 season to improve their roster?
pairs.answer: The Pistons acquired Ben Wallace and Chucky Atkins from the Orlando Magic, Cedric Ceballos, Dana Barros, and John Wallace from the Dallas Mavericks, and Billy Owens from the Milwaukee Bucks. Mid-season, they traded Jerome Williams and Eric Montross to the Toronto Raptors for Corliss Williamson. They also traded Ceballos to the Miami Heat in November.

pairs.question: How did Ben Wallace perform in the 2000–01 NBA season, and what were his career-high stats?
pairs.answer: Ben Wallace averaged 6.4 points, 13.2 rebounds, 1.3 steals, and 2.3 blocks per game. His defensive contributions earned him a fifth-place tie in Defensive Player of the Year voting and a ninth-place tie in Most Improved Player voting.

pairs.question: Which Detroit Pistons player was selected for the NBA All-Star Game in 2001, and what was their selection?
pairs.answer: Jerry Stackhouse was selected for the 2001 NBA All-Star Game as a member of the Eastern Conference All-Star team, marking his second and final All-Star appearance.

pairs.question: What changes were made to the Detroit Pistons' coaching staff and roster after the 2000–01 season?
pairs.answer: Following the season, head coach George Irvine was fired. Players leaving included Joe Smith (re-signed with the Minnesota Timberwolves), Mateen Cleaves (traded to the Sacramento Kings), John Wallace (dealt to the Phoenix Suns), and Billy Owens (retired).

pairs.question: How did the Detroit Pistons perform at home during the 2000–01 regular season in terms of attendance?
pairs.answer: The Pistons had an attendance of 607,323 at The Palace of Auburn Hills for home games, ranking 22nd in the NBA for home-game attendance.
